title: 1.1: Reports that a document appears immediately in search results after API title update, e.g., searching for the new title within seconds returns 1 result.
1.2: Reports that no background task gets triggered during API document updates, e.g., qcluster logs remain unchanged or Redis queue length stays at 0.
1.3: Identifies 'python manage.py document_index reindex' as the command to force reconciliation between database state, e.g., running this command rebuilds the entire search index.
1.4: Reports that the reindex command runs synchronously in the management command process, e.g., qcluster logs remain unchanged, or Redis queue length remains 0 after reindex completes.
1.5: Reports that the reindex command displays a tqdm progress bar during rebuild, e.g., output shows percentage completion from 0% to 100%.
1.6: States that the system auto-creates an empty index after index deletion, e.g., search returns 0 results or index directory is recreated containing initial Whoosh files.
1.7: Reports index reconstruction timing for a small document set, e.g., approximately 1 second total, processing rate of hundreds of documents per second.
1.9: Reports that a direct SQL title update leaves the search index stale, e.g., searching for the SQL-updated title returns 0 results.
